name: Cracked Wheat Carrot Loaf
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, pour boiling water over cracked wheat; let stand for 15 minutes. Drain excess water and set aside.
2. Dissolve yeast in warm water.
3. In a large bowl, combine milk, margarine, brown sugar, salt, carrot, and egg. Add dissolved yeast and cracked wheat. The margarine may not melt completely.
4. In a small bowl, combine 1 cup of the all-purpose flour and oat bran cereal; add to yeast mixture. Add enough remaining all-purpose flour to make a moderately stiff dough.
5. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface. Knead about 10 minutes or until dough is smooth and elastic.
6. Shape into a ball and grease a large bowl. Place dough in bowl, turning once to coat the surface. Cover; let rise in a warm place for about 1 1/2 hours, or until nearly double in size.
7. Grease a 9-inch by 5-inch loaf pan or coat with vegetable oil spray.
8. Punch down dough; shape into a loaf. Place in prepared pan. Cover; let rise in a warm place for about 1 hour, or until nearly doubled in size.
9. Meanwhile, preheat oven to 375°F.
10. Bake 25-30 minutes, shielding the crust with aluminum foil after 20 minutes of baking.
11. Remove from pan and cool on a wire rack.
Food Exchanges per serving: 2 STARCH EXCHANGES
CHO: 30g; PRO: 4g; FAT: 1g; CAL: 146
Low-sodium diets: Omit salt. Substitute unsalted margarine.
